plot:entries:p-receiptly.title: Parse merchant, total, and tax from mobile receipt photos
Add review screen for low-confidence OCR fields
Export selected receipts as CSV for accountants
Built local receipt inbox with drag-and-drop upload
plot:entries:p-receiptly.deliverable: Users can drop JPG, PNG, or PDF receipts into a dated inbox. Files persist locally and preview instantly.

plot:entries:p-receiptly.title: Added category chips for meals, travel, software, and office
plot:entries:p-receiptly.deliverable: Chips save per receipt and can be edited inline. Filtering by category is now fast enough for weekly cleanup.

plot:entries:p-receiptly.title: Connect Gmail import for emailed receipts
plot:entries:p-receiptly.parkedReason: OAuth review is too much surface area before the manual workflow is proven.

plot:entries:p-receiptly.title: Detect duplicate receipts across PDF exports
plot:entries:p-receiptly.parkedReason: Useful later, but current beta users only upload 20-40 receipts at a time.

plot:entries:p-receiptly.body: Stay local-first for v1. People are more willing to test a receipt tool when tax documents never leave the browser.
Users care more about correcting totals quickly than perfect OCR. A fast review loop beats invisible automation.

plot:entries:p-promptdeck.title: Ship prompt version history with diff view
Add keyboard shortcut to duplicate a prompt card
Create template pack for product research prompts
Built board view for prompt collections
plot:entries:p-promptdeck.deliverable: Prompt cards can be grouped by use case, reordered, and opened in a focused editor.

plot:entries:p-promptdeck.title: Added run notes after each prompt test
plot:entries:p-promptdeck.deliverable: Each run stores model, input context, and a short result note so good prompts are easier to reuse.

plot:entries:p-promptdeck.title: One-click publishable prompt gallery
plot:entries:p-promptdeck.parkedReason: Public sharing can wait until the private workflow feels indispensable.

plot:entries:p-promptdeck.title: Team workspace billing
plot:entries:p-promptdeck.parkedReason: Solo users are the current target. Billing would slow the product loop.

plot:entries:p-promptdeck.body: Make the core unit a prompt card, not a chat thread. People want reusable building blocks, not another inbox of conversations.
The first five beta users all asked where the 'last good version' went. Versioning is not a power-user feature here.

plot:entries:p-mealmate.title: Generate grocery list from a 5-day meal plan
Add pantry exclusions before recipe suggestions
Fix recipe cards overflowing on small screens
Added drag-to-swap meals between weekdays
plot:entries:p-mealmate.deliverable: Users can move dinners around the week without regenerating the whole plan.

plot:entries:p-mealmate.title: Built dietary preference setup
plot:entries:p-mealmate.deliverable: Vegetarian, gluten-free, high-protein, and kid-friendly preferences now steer suggestions.

plot:entries:p-mealmate.title: Integrate Instacart cart handoff
plot:entries:p-mealmate.parkedReason: Partnership/API work is too early before retention is validated.

plot:entries:p-mealmate.title: Nutrition macro dashboard
plot:entries:p-mealmate.parkedReason: Could pull the app toward calorie tracking, which is not the current promise.

plot:entries:p-mealmate.body: Position as 'reduce dinner decisions' instead of 'optimize nutrition'. The former is the pain people immediately recognize.
Regenerating the whole week feels wasteful. People want to lock two meals they like and only replace the awkward slots.

plot:entries:p-focusforge.title: Add blocklist schedules for workdays vs weekends
Show session recap after a completed focus block
Fix timer drift after laptop sleep
Shipped strict mode for deep work sessions
plot:entries:p-focusforge.deliverable: Blocked sites stay blocked until the timer ends. Escape hatch requires typing the session goal back.

plot:entries:p-focusforge.title: Added weekly focus streaks
plot:entries:p-focusforge.deliverable: Dashboard now shows completed blocks, longest streak, and missed days without feeling punitive.

plot:entries:p-focusforge.title: Sync blocklists across devices
plot:entries:p-focusforge.parkedReason: Useful, but local Chrome storage is enough for the first extension-only release.

plot:entries:p-focusforge.title: Ambient sound player
plot:entries:p-focusforge.parkedReason: Nice-to-have that risks turning the product into a generic focus app.

plot:entries:p-focusforge.body: The product should intervene before distraction, not report after the fact. Blocking and planning matter more than analytics.
A tiny pre-session goal makes strict mode feel intentional instead of hostile.

plot:entries:p-launchnotes.title: Generate release note draft from merged PR titles
Add tone presets: concise, customer-facing, technical
Fix markdown export losing nested bullet indentation
Built changelog editor with before/after preview
plot:entries:p-launchnotes.deliverable: Users can edit generated copy and compare against the raw commit summary in one screen.

plot:entries:p-launchnotes.title: Added Linear issue import by project key
plot:entries:p-launchnotes.deliverable: Import pulls issue title, status, assignee, and labels into a draft release bucket.

plot:entries:p-launchnotes.title: Auto-publish to Slack and Discord
plot:entries:p-launchnotes.parkedReason: Export is enough for launch. Integrations can follow if teams ask repeatedly.

plot:entries:p-launchnotes.title: Public changelog hosting
plot:entries:p-launchnotes.parkedReason: Would require auth, teams, and billing earlier than needed.

plot:entries:p-launchnotes.body: Start as a drafting tool, not a publishing platform. The immediate pain is turning messy shipping notes into clear copy.
Founders want fewer adjectives and more concrete customer impact. The best generated notes mention what changed and who benefits.

plot:entries:p-tinycrm.title: Add follow-up reminders to each contact card
Import contacts from a CSV with column mapping
Add empty states for zero leads and zero follow-ups
Built pipeline columns for Lead, Warm, Proposal, Closed
plot:entries:p-tinycrm.deliverable: Cards drag between columns and keep last-contact notes visible.

plot:entries:p-tinycrm.title: Added quick note capture from the keyboard
plot:entries:p-tinycrm.deliverable: Pressing N opens a note composer on the selected contact without leaving the board.

plot:entries:p-tinycrm.title: Email inbox sync
plot:entries:p-tinycrm.parkedReason: Manual notes are enough for the lightweight CRM promise.

plot:entries:p-tinycrm.title: Team permissions
plot:entries:p-tinycrm.parkedReason: This is for solo consultants first. Team features would dilute the UX.

plot:entries:p-tinycrm.body: Optimize for the weekly review ritual. A tiny CRM succeeds if it tells me who needs attention today.
People do not want another Salesforce. They want fewer dropped conversations and one place to remember context.

plot:entries:p-habittrail.title: Add weekly habit review with keep/change/drop prompts
Build calendar heatmap for habit streaks
Fix timezone bug that marks late-night check-ins as tomorrow
Added flexible targets for weekly habits
plot:entries:p-habittrail.deliverable: Users can set '3 times per week' instead of daily-only streaks. Misses feel less punishing.

plot:entries:p-habittrail.title: Built private notes on each check-in
plot:entries:p-habittrail.deliverable: Each completion can include a one-line note about context, mood, or obstacle.

plot:entries:p-habittrail.title: Social accountability groups
plot:entries:p-habittrail.parkedReason: The product should work as a private tracker before adding social pressure.

plot:entries:p-habittrail.title: Apple Health integration
plot:entries:p-habittrail.parkedReason: Useful for fitness habits, but too narrow for the first public version.

plot:entries:p-habittrail.body: Avoid guilt-based copy. Habit tools already make people feel behind; this one should help people restart cleanly.
Weekly targets make people more honest. Daily streaks look impressive but cause silent churn after one missed day.
